paper_title: Archaeological Site and Gorilla Occurrence Data

methods.method_name: Data Collection for Archaeological Sites and Gorilla Occurrence
methods.description: This method involves collecting data on archaeological sites and gorilla occurrences to study the environment in which ancient people lived and its relation to the host switch probability of malaria Plasmodium species.

steps.description: Pliocene and the early Pleistocene occurrence of fossil hominin species and the accompanying fossil fauna were gained from the Fossilworks database.
steps.searchable_summary: download fossil hominin species data from Fossilworks database

steps.description: The Lupenbam culture-related archaeological site data were gained from Taylor (2011, 2016).
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve Lupenbam culture archaeological site data from Taylor's publications

methods.description: This method estimates the plausible environmental requirements of fossil species to characterize ancient taphonomical conditions related to dryness/wetness, which affects malaria prevalence.

steps.description: Estimate plausible environmental requirements of the fossil species based on land characteristics and environmental patterns.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze land characteristics to estimate environmental requirements of fossil species

steps.description: Characterize sites as dry terrestrial, mesic, or wetlands based on environmental requirements and malaria prevalence.
steps.searchable_summary: classify sites into dry, mesic, or wetlands based on environmental data
